Investor Anatomy
US Liquidity and Monetary Conditions
Home
US Liquidity and Monetary Conditions
Synthesises Federal Reserve liquidity measures, money supply, real-rate signals and SOFR positioning to assess the current US monetary and liquidity regime.

Regime Assessment:

  • Regime: Transitional / Fragile Balance
  • Regime Confidence Index: Medium Confidence
  • Stability: Fragile

Why This Regime:

  • The Liquidity Composite is currently in a persistent Neutral state (1).
  • Real Interest Rate Trends remain Bearish and restrictive, creating a structural headwind (2).
  • M2 Money Supply growth has stabilized within baseline neutral thresholds (3).
  • Weighting prioritized the structural restrictiveness of real rates over tactical liquidity stabilization.
  • The Liquidity Composite was downweighted due to internal conflict between RRP and Reserve signals (1).

Alignment & Tensions:

  • Liquidity and M2 signals both confirm a Neutral baseline for the monetary base (1)(3).
  • A major tension exists between neutral liquidity volumes and restrictive real interest rates (2).
  • Speculative positioning shows divergence between short-term rate-hike bets and medium-term cooling expectations (4)(5).
  • Tensions do not overturn the regime call as they define the current lack of clear directional momentum.

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant scenario: Range-biased price paths under neutral liquidity and restrictive real costs.
  • Primary upside risk: Liquidity expansion if M2 3-month momentum exceeds 1.0% (3).
  • Primary downside risk: Contraction if the 10-year real yield continues to rise above its trend (2).

What Would Change the Regime:

  • A Liquidity Composite move beyond +/- 0.75 would signal a directional regime transition (1).
  • A decline in the 10-year real yield to below 1.77% would invalidate the current restrictive bias (2).
Inputs
Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite Signal

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the Liquidity Composite of 0.028 and neutral signals across M2 and Federal Reserve assets, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Regime shift (1).
  • The signal is currently in a Neutral regime, having stabilized near the zero-line after a period of mild contractionary pressure (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • A Composite Score (LC) between -0.75 and 0.75 defines a Neutral liquidity regime (1).
  • Falling Overnight Reverse Repo (ON RRP) usage is interpreted as an incremental easing impulse regardless of balance sheet size (1).
  • Rising Reserve Balances are considered supportive of lower short-term funding volatility (1).
  • Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite Signal (Latest: 2026-03-31) (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the stabilization of M2 growth and the exhaustion of ON RRP drainage, which previously provided an easing impulse (1).
  • Momentum has shifted from negative to slightly positive, indicating a stabilization of the monetary base (1).
  • Internal Conflict: The RRP signal remains Bullish (easing) while Fed Assets and Reserves have transitioned to Neutral, creating a mixed liquidity profile (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A Composite Score move beyond +/- 0.75 would signal a transition to a directional expansionary or contractionary regime (1).
  • Signal stability is high, as the composite has remained within the Neutral band for over 24 months (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Neutral liquidity conditions persist as Fed QT and M2 growth find equilibrium.
  • Upside risk: Accelerated RRP drainage or a pivot to QE triggers a broad-based liquidity expansion.
  • Downside risk: Sudden contraction in reserve balances leads to systemic liquidity withdrawal.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) as the signal relies on 3-month momentum and year-over-year changes (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; although a primary regime filter, current mixed signals reduce aggregate conviction.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the US Liquidity & Monetary Conditions dimension by quantifying the net expansion of the monetary base (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Measures private sector credit availability and settlement liquidity for depository institutions (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with credit spreads and the USD Index to confirm systemic funding stress or ease (1).

Regime Context:

  • Persistent Neutral regime; the current state has been maintained since mid-2023 (1).
  • Direction of change: Stabilizing near the 0.00 level.

Model Limitations:

  • M2 reporting lags and sensitivity to Treasury General Account (TGA) volatility can create noise (1).

Data & References:

  • Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite Signal (2026-03-31) (1).
  • Influential datapoints: Liquidity Composite (0.028), RRP 3M Momentum (-100%) (1).
  • Public datasets: 10-Year Real TIPS yields and the USD Index would improve regime confirmation (1).
M2 Money Supply Signal

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement (mandatory): Given M2SL_YoY at 4.29% and M2SL_3Month_Change at 0.82%, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation (1).
  • The signal is currently in a Neutral regime as both level and momentum components sit within baseline thresholds (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium, Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence, and Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable, Threshold Proximity: Near, and Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime classification uses fixed thresholds for M2SL_YoY (Bullish > 6.0%, Bearish < 2.0%) (1).
  • Short-term momentum is governed by M2SL_3M_Change (Bullish > 1.0%, Bearish < -1.0%) (1).
  • The Overall_Regime is determined by a "worst-of" risk-control aggregation of level and momentum states (1).
  • Dataset: M2 Money Stock (M2SL), latest observation 2026-01-01 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • M2SL_YoY (4.29%) sits centrally in the 2.0%–6.0% Neutral range (1).
  • 3-month momentum (0.82%) is currently stabilizing below the +1.0% acceleration threshold (1).
  • There are no internal tensions as both metrics suggest a balanced liquidity environment (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A 3-month momentum print exceeding 1.0% would trigger a Bullish regime shift (1).
  • Stability is high, with four consecutive months of Neutral readings following the mid-2025 expansion (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant; risks balanced.
  • Upside risk: Liquidity acceleration if 3-month momentum breaks above 1.0% (1).
  • Downside risk: Liquidity contraction if YoY growth falls below the 2.0% floor (1).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months); signal reflects medium-term liquidity trends (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; serves as a fundamental input for conditioning risk tilts in metals and cyclicals (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the macro liquidity dimension by tracking the broad money supply (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Neutrality implies stable capital availability, neither providing an impulse nor a headwind to risk assets (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with Federal Reserve policy signals and inflation composites to frame the "risk-on/off" environment (1).

Regime Context:

  • The signal is in a persistent Neutral regime following a transition from Bullish in late 2025 (1).
  • Direction of change: Stabilising within the neutral zone (1).

Model Limitations:

  • M2 may lag real-time financial conditions; thresholds are baseline and may require overrides in extreme macro environments (1).
  • Does not account for velocity of money or non-M2 liquidity components (1).

Data & References:

  • Dataset: FRED M2SL (M2 Money Stock), Latest: 2026-01-01 (1).
  • Most influential: M2SL_3Month_Change (0.82%) sitting near the Bullish threshold (1).
  • Federal Reserve Liquidity Composite and M1 velocity data would improve depth.
Real Interest Rate Trend Signal

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a 10-year real yield (DFII10) of 1.85% exceeding its 1.82% six-month trend, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not mean reversion (1).
  • The signal is currently classified as Bearish, indicating restrictive real financial conditions (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Real yield > 6-month moving average by > 0.05% implies tightening real-rate pressure at the margin (1).
  • Real yield within ± 0.05% of trend indicates stable conditions and a Neutral regime (1).
  • Observation date: 2026-03-01.

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the persistence of the 10-year TIPS yield at 1.85%, maintaining a restrictive gap above the moving average (1).
  • The signal shows directional stability, remaining in the Bearish regime for three consecutive periods (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A decline in the 10-year real yield to below 1.77% (Trend - 0.05%) would shift the bias to Neutral or Bullish.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Restrictive real rates persist, weighing on long-duration asset valuations.
  • Upside risk: A rapid deceleration in nominal yields or a spike in inflation expectations (breakevens) that compresses real rates toward the trend.
  • Downside risk: Further tightening of Fed policy or upward revisions to growth expectations pushing real yields significantly higher.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) based on the six-month trend filter window (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; this provides a foundational "cost of capital" overlay for growth-sensitive signals.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the Monetary Conditions dimension by measuring the stance of real restrictiveness (1).
  • Economic mechanism: Elevated real rates increase real borrowing costs, potentially dampening capital expenditure and tightening financial conditions.
  • Cycle position: Not determined.

Regime Context:

  • Persistent Bearish regime; the current state has been maintained since January 2026 (1).
  • The transition is currently stabilising within the restrictive zone.

Model Limitations:

  • Lagging behavior due to the moving average window and potential distortions from TIPS liquidity premia during stress (1).

Data & References:

  • Latest Observation: 2026-03-01 (1).
  • Influential Datapoints: DFII10 (1.85) and DFII10_Trend (1.82) (1).
  • Public datasets for improvement: 5-year TIPS yields (DFII5) and 10-year Breakeven Inflation (T10YIE) (1).
SOF 3M - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Speculator net positioning of -3.9% and a Speculator z-score of 0.21, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Compressed volatility, where the dominant risk is Data uncertainty, not trend exhaustion.
  • The signal is in a "Normal" state within a Balanced_Short_Bias regime, indicating a stabilization of positioning after a period of higher tension (1)(2).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: Moderate.

Methodology Applied:

  • Speculator z-scores identify crowding; values ±2.0 trigger exhaustion or squeeze risk alerts (1).
  • Directional regime bias logic: Falling positioning values indicate a shift toward tightening or restrictive policy expectations (1).
  • Positioning Tension Score: Measures policy narrative instability; low scores suggest a stable market consensus (1).
  • Latest observation from SOFR-3M CoT data dated 2026-03-10 (2).

Key Dynamics:

  • Speculator net positioning remains negative (-3.9% of OI), and the z-score (0.21) reflects mean-reverting stability from previous extremes (2).
  • Momentum has stabilized, with a 4-week flow of -0.4 showing negligible directional pressure (2).
  • Low Tension Score (0.40) and zero risk flags indicate a lack of immediate positioning fragility (2).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A Speculator z-score breach of ±2.0 or a sharp multi-week flow reversal (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued range-bound trading as positioning aligns with current Fed guidance.
  • Upside risk: Dovish data surprise triggers a short covering rally, though current low short crowding limits convexity.
  • Downside risk: Persistent hawkish data forces a breakout of net short contracts toward historical extremes.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks): Flow and tension metrics are optimized for near-term reversal and setup quality assessment (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; current "Normal" state provides a stable baseline for broader monetary regime aggregation.

Macro Relevance:

  • Monetary Policy Expectations: Measures market consensus on the Fed's pace and terminal rate (1).
  • Economic Mechanism: Speculative reduction indicates a "higher-for-longer" narrative reinforcement as easing premiums are priced out (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with inflation and labor signals to validate whether policy expectations match fundamental data.

Regime Context:

  • The signal transitioned from a Balanced_Long_Bias in December 2025 to a persistent Balanced_Short_Bias since early 2026 (2).
  • Direction of change: Stabilising at neutral levels as speculative tension dissipates.

Model Limitations:

  • Weekly reporting lag may obscure rapid intra-week policy repricing events (1).
  • Commercial hedger data reflects structural positioning rather than directional policy conviction (1).

Data & References:

  • SOFR-3M Commitment of Traders (CoT) report, latest date 2026-03-10 (2).
  • Speculator Net %OI (-3.9%) and Tension Score (0.40) are primary drivers of the "Normal" classification (2).
  • CME FedWatch and FOMC Dot Plot updates would improve the reliability of policy narrative interpretation.
SOF 5Y - Individual Market Analysis

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given Spec_Net_%OI of 17.3% and a Spec_zscore of 0.708, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Mean reversion. (1)
  • The signal currently occupies a Balanced_Long_Bias regime, indicating a stabilising expectation for lower medium-term rates after a period of volatility. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown. (1)

Methodology Applied:

  • Rising long positioning is interpreted as a consensus expectation for disinflationary or economic slowdown regimes. (1)
  • Aligned flow (positive) and net positioning (positive) indicate trend expansion rather than exhaustion. (1)
  • Transition or mean-reversion regimes are triggered when Speculator z-scores exceed a +/- 2.0 threshold. (1)
  • 5 YEAR ERIS SOFR SWAP data, latest observation: 2026-03-10. (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is a positive 4-week Positioning Flow of +1.3, which confirms the existing Spec_Net_%OI of 17.3%. (1)
  • Positioning is stabilising following a transition from high tension (z > 1.0) in January to a "Normal" synthetic state. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A multi-week flow reversal that opposes the net long direction would signal conviction fading. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued gradual long-build as participants position for disinflation. (1)
  • Upside risk: Accelerated long-build triggered by clear signals of economic cooling. (1)
  • Downside risk: Sharp liquidation if reflationary data forces a repricing of the neutral rate (r*). (1)

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months), reflecting strategic investor conviction in medium-term macro beliefs. (1)
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, as the signal is currently within normal historical bounds without extreme crowding. (1)

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the medium-term policy transmission and growth-inflation balance. (1)
  • The long bias implies an expectation of easing monetary conditions or fading demand impulse. (1)
  • Cycle position: Not determined.

Regime Context:

  • Persistent transition from a short-bias in February to a stabilising long-bias in March. (1)
  • Direction of change: Stabilising. (1)

Model Limitations:

  • Reflects strategic asymmetry rather than precise timing for tactical FOMC events. (1)
  • CoT data includes a weekly reporting lag. (1)

Data & References:

  • Latest Data: 2026-03-10; Speculator Net %OI (17.3%), Flow_4w (+1.3). (1)
  • Influential points: Shift from Balanced_Short_Bias (2026-02-24) to Balanced_Long_Bias. (1)
Investor Anatomy - US Liquidity and Monetary Conditions - Not Investment Advice
The information presented is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute financial or investment advice. It has been prepared without regard to individual objectives, financial situation, or needs. You should consider whether it is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent advice where necessary.
